commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR restyles skill and plugin detail pages, adds sidebar download activity UI, skill-card/code/file/diff/version polish, updates tests, and records the detail-page design direction in a new spec note.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR blockers: GitHub CI logs at f931af7a show the TypeScript and Vitest failures, and source/history inspection shows the activity metric tooltip was reintroduced after current main removed it. I did not rerun tests locally because this review kept the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blockers are narrow, mechanical source/test repairs on the PR branch; broader design acceptance should stay with maintainers after checks pass.

target.title: feat(web): polish skill and plugin detail pages
target.body:
## Summary

- Align skill and plugin detail pages on a shared mobile/desktop layout: hero taxonomy, install surface, sidebar metadata, tab chrome, README/skill-card rendering, files/versions/diff panels, and related polish from `specs/plugin-skill-detail-polish-iteration.md`.
- Add download activity graphs in the sidebar, skill-card preview, markdown code-block tooling, and plugin-specific detail affordances (categorize alert placement, catalog metadata dialog, version panels).
- Fix SSR hydration mismatches on detail pages by keeping stable mobile markup (`<details>` + CSS desktop layout) and syncing Shiki theme selection with `useSyncExternalStore`; complete tabpanel ARIA for Files/Versions.
- Align skill/plugin hero summary rows (Star + Install band height), compact owner management toolbar actions, and mobile install CLI copy layout.
- Plugin sidebar polish: metadata always visible on desktop; mobile master tabs **About | Stats**; plugin download inline with the downloads count when no activity graph is shown, and as the last sidebar item when a graph is present.
- Remove accidental local `.proof/pr-2780-screenshots` artifacts from the branch and ignore `.proof/` going forward.

**Scope:** frontend only (`src/` + spec note). No Convex/backend changes in this PR.


## Preview

Vercel preview (prod Convex) — latest deploy (`f931af7a`):

- https://clawhub-design-ote0uky9n-victor-brzezowskis-projects.vercel.app/plugins/@openclaw/whatsapp
- https://clawhub-design-ote0uky9n-victor-brzezowskis-projects.vercel.app/plugins/@openclaw/codex
- https://clawhub-design-ote0uky9n-victor-brzezowskis-projects.vercel.app/ivangdavila/self-improving
- https://clawhub-design-ote0uky9n-victor-brzezowskis-projects.vercel.app/pskoett/self-improving-agent

Note: Diff tab may stay on "Loading diff…" on the Vercel preview because Monaco is blocked by preview CSP; local dev and production are unaffected.

## Screenshots

- [x] Screenshots/recordings attached
- **Full gallery (68 captures):** https://velvet-mustard-yce4.here.now/
- Captured from production `clawhub.ai` (before) vs Vercel preview (after), light + dark, one viewport per tab (1440×1100 with breathing room).

## Behavioural Proof

- Local worktree review at `http://127.0.0.1:16456` with seeded fixtures + prod Convex preview URLs above.
- Verified mobile master tabs, deferred stats sections, install switcher, categorize alert placement, and sidebar download graphs on the listed routes.
- Hydration fixes verified by keeping identical SSR/client DOM for deferred sections and theme-aware markdown rendering.

## Security / Trust Impact

- [x] No security/trust impact
- [ ] Security/trust impact explained

## Data / Deploy Impact

- [x] No data/deploy impact
- [ ] Data/deploy impact explained

## Verification

- [x] `bun run ci:static`
- [x] Focused tests for touched behavior:
  - `VITE_CONVEX_URL=https://example.invalid bun run vitest run src/__tests__/package-detail-route.test.tsx src/components/SkillHeader.test.tsx`
- [x] `bun run ci:unit`
- [x] Broader gate: `bun run ci:types-build`
- [x] Other: merged latest `upstream/main` before opening PR
target.author: vyctorbrzezowski

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
